question_text: Two forces act on a box sliding across a frictionless floor at constant velocity: a forward push of 25 N and air resistance. The box has a mass of 10 kg and is moving at a constant 2 m/s. Calculate the magnitude of the air resistance force and explain why the box does not accelerate even though a push force is being applied.
correct_answer: Air resistance = 25 N. The box does not accelerate because the forces are balanced (push = air resistance), so the resultant force is zero.
explanation: When an object moves at constant velocity, its acceleration is zero, which means the resultant force must be zero. The forward push of 25 N must be balanced by the backward air resistance force of 25 N. Balanced forces mean no acceleration, even though forces are acting.
